meta.description: Validates an existing prognostic risk signature on an external bulk expression cohort with survival outcomes. Produces risk scores, Kaplan-Meier curves, risk distribution plots, expression heatmap, and time-dependent ROC curves from a fixed model coefficient file.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated risk scores, p-values, AUC values, or survival statistics across all 7 inputs; all outputs derived from actual survival computation and time-dependent ROC with fixed seed

practice_boundaries.detail: Research Use Notice explicitly states the skill is for research and validation only; de-identification requirement stated; no diagnostic conclusions or treatment recommendations produced

methodological_ground.detail: Median-split risk stratification, log-rank KM comparison, and IPCW time-dependent ROC are all standard and correct for external validation of a prognostic signature; roc_times-in-years documentation prevents a common unit-mismatch error

code_usability.detail: scripts/main.R and 5 helper scripts are syntactically complete; validate_color, parse_csv_arg, and validate_choice all called at startup; analysis.log and run_parameters.tsv preserved even on error; no infinite loops or missing core dependencies

functional_suitability.note: Complete 4-step validation pipeline with all 6 output plots fully documented; roc_times-in-years note prevents the most common unit-mismatch error; Research Use Notice proactively addresses de-identification requirements

reliability.note: Most sophisticated error handling of all 6 audited skills: analysis.log with memory checkpoints, run_parameters.tsv preserved on error, graceful partial-save logic, SKILL_PKG_VERSION for version constraint failures, 9 distinct SKILL_* error codes

performance_context.note: Only skill with a complete references/ directory on disk (algorithm.md, troubleshooting.md, cli-guide.md, baseline-run.md, project-structure.md); full progressive disclosure design realized; SKILL.md 308 lines is the longest but structured with clear section headers

agent_usability.note: Multiple When to Read External Files table with 11 entries covering every operational scenario; roc_times documentation with explicit years-regardless-of-unit note is exceptional; one minor gap: the overwrite flag behavior could be more prominently explained in the Quick Start workflow

human_usability.note: Research Use Notice positioned prominently before When to Use; When Not to Use covers 5 distinct cases; natural trigger language for 'external validation' is well-calibrated

security.note: Research Use Notice plus de-identification requirement; validate_color prevents injection via color arguments; normalizePath(mustWork=TRUE) on user paths could expose filesystem structure in error messages but acceptable in local research context

maintainability.note: Six scripts with clear single responsibilities (plotting.R, io.R, functions.R, run_analysis.R, utils.R, main.R); baseline-run.md for regression testing; refresh_example_output.R for test bundle regeneration; project-structure.md for onboarding

agent_specific.note: Best trigger precision of all 6 audited skills; When to Use / When Not to Use both present; references/ directory enables genuine progressive disclosure; --overwrite flag prevents accidental data loss; composable clean outputs; idempotent with --overwrite TRUE

inputs.label: Five ROC time points including potentially out-of-range values
inputs.note: roc_colors recycled correctly; one gap: max follow-up pre-validation at argument stage rather than inside ROC

assertions.text: roc_times values validated as positive numeric at argument parsing stage
assertions.note: parse_csv_arg and any(roc_times <= 0) check confirmed in main.R

assertions.text: SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER or SKILL_ANALYSIS_ERROR emitted for roc_times exceeding max follow-up
assertions.note: SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER documented for impossible ROC time points; fails cleanly

assertions.text: roc_colors automatically recycled when fewer colors provided than time points
assertions.note: rep(roc_colors, length.out=length(roc_times)) logic confirmed in main.R

assertions.text: Pre-validation check confirms all roc_times are below max observed follow-up before ROC computation begins
assertions.note: Validation of positive values occurs at parse time but pre-check against max(OS.time) does not happen until inside the ROC computation step; error is caught but later than ideal

assertions.text: run_parameters.tsv records all 5 roc_times values for complete debugging context
assertions.note: All comma-separated roc_times captured in parameter record

inputs.label: Model genes absent from expression matrix
inputs.note: Missing genes detected at Step 2 of workflow; analysis.log captures which genes are absent; clean failure

assertions.text: Missing model genes detected and reported before risk score computation begins
assertions.note: Workflow Step 2 documents: 'Check all model genes exist in the expression matrix'

assertions.text: analysis.log captures which specific model genes are absent from expression matrix
assertions.note: Runtime log with processing steps captures gene matching outcome

assertions.text: Appropriate SKILL_* error code emitted for missing model genes
assertions.note: SKILL_MISSING_COLUMNS or SKILL_INVALID_DATA covers missing gene scenario in Error Handling table

assertions.text: No partial risk scores computed when any required model gene is missing
assertions.note: Clean failure before linear predictor computation; all-or-nothing gene matching

assertions.text: SKILL_INVALID_DATA handling for duplicate genes includes deduplication guidance
assertions.note: Error handling provides concrete deduplication examples: dplyr::distinct() and highest mean expression approach

key_strengths: roc_times documentation explicitly stating 'always in years regardless of --time_unit' prevents the most common unit-mismatch error in time-dependent ROC analysis
Only skill in the audited set with a complete references/ directory on disk, achieving full progressive disclosure with algorithm.md, troubleshooting.md, baseline-run.md, and project-structure.md
Research Use Notice prominently placed before When to Use, with explicit de-identification requirement, is the strongest responsible-use design of all 6 audited skills
--overwrite flag protection prevents silent data loss from accidental reruns into existing output directories
analysis.log with memory checkpoints plus run_parameters.tsv preserved even on error enables complete post-hoc debugging

recommendations.title: roc_times out-of-range validation occurs inside ROC computation rather than at startup
recommendations.problem: Validation that roc_times values do not exceed maximum observed follow-up time is not performed at the argument-parsing stage in main.R; the error is caught inside the ROC computation function after data has already been loaded and processed.
recommendations.root_cause: Maximum observed follow-up is only known after reading the clinical file; a pre-validation step would require reading the file during argument validation rather than deferring to the analysis stage.
recommendations.fix: After reading the clinical file in Step 2, compute max_followup_years and validate all roc_times against it: if any(roc_times > max_followup_years) stop_skill('SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER', sprintf('roc_times values %s exceed maximum observed follow-up of %.1f years', paste(roc_times[roc_times > max_followup_years], collapse=', '), max_followup_years)). This provides a clearer error with the actual max follow-up value.
